  • [ - ] joshualiley72 - member since 24-Nov-20

    Everything points to a Morocco win except for the quality on paper between the 2 sides and the fact that African teams never make it this far. The GK who wins the golden glove is often the one who wins the tournament, and given Emi Martinez has a save percentage of 44% and Lloris hasn't kept a clean sheet all tournament, you'd fancy Bounou or Livakovic to win it. Onto that, France have conceded a goal every game, while no one has scored past Morocco except themselves, only once. Morocco will make up the majority of the fans in the stadium. France would be very good on the counter but are unlikely to get that here and so may have to rely on crosses into Giroud. Morocco 1-0 France. Attack wins you games, defence wins you titles

    well, morocco could very well do what they did before. its just that france are more complete and attacking. 2 deadly wingers linked by griezmann as a 10 and giroud with good quality at finishing, passing, first touch, & in aerial situations. of course, portugal bombarded them similarly and they were able to hold on. morocco will need their first team starters in top form, their 2nd team forwards & defenders were somewhat exposed (cheddira most notably) several times - no depth. frances tactics are on point but if amrabat could deal with bruno, bernardo & felix im sure he can deal with rabiot & tchouameni. theyve still yet to be scored on. we thought the previous teams would be too much for morocco just like we do now, but they werent. can they do it again? pretty rare for a team to go all the way like this esp being such an underdog, but not unheard of. my feeling is 2-1, my brain says 1-0 either way. no extra time

  • [ - ] Zoltan - member since 15-Feb-20

    Don't get fooled, in reality we have two African teams here with all the dire consequences this might mean. For one, we have at our hands a defensive nightmare in which scoring the first goal will be key. For another, the best goalkeepers and best defences of this World Cup will do their best to neutralise the attacking prowess of the other team. Unless the coaches do something special in the light of the above observations, we are in for an UNDER 2.5 goals game. What I mean by "something special" here is either practicing set pieces, or practicing long range shots (much like the one Tchuamenie surprised England with). Once one of the teams manages to score relatively early on (say in the 17th minute like against England), then we are in for an over 2.5 goals game, because then the other team opens up and will become vulnerable for counterattack. Alternately, we can also go for BTTS after the first early goal.
